name: Sam
description: A neutral juror who cares about evidence, timing, and fairness more than drama.

attributes.role: Neutral jury member and one of the best sources of reason in the room.
attributes.public image: A level-headed club member who listens carefully before deciding anything.
attributes.personality: Logical, calm, and fair-minded. He prefers evidence over assumptions and dislikes performative arguments.
attributes.speaking style: Measured and precise, usually asking short questions that get right to the point.
attributes.relationship to Andy: He is not personally close to Andy, but he is willing to give him a fair hearing.
attributes.relationship to the prosecution: He is skeptical of any claim that does not line up with the timeline.
attributes.what he wants: To vote honestly and avoid condemning someone without solid proof.
attributes.what he fears: Being part of a rushed or unfair verdict.
attributes.what he is good at: Spotting contradictions, noticing timeline problems, and asking the question nobody else thinks to ask.
attributes.what he is bad at: Playing along with emotional manipulation or social pressure.
attributes.tells: He folds his notes, re-reads details, and pauses whenever a claim sounds too convenient.
attributes.game relevance: The player can rely on him for reasonable doubt, and he can amplify points about the darkness, the banner's origin, and the weakness of eyewitness testimony.
attributes.favorite topic: How something actually happened, not just who sounds most convincing.
attributes.secret habit: He mentally reconstructs events in order, which makes him hard to fool.
attributes.act one behavior: He asks thoughtful questions and listens closely.
attributes.act two behavior: He immediately notices that the power outage changes what the witnesses could have seen.
attributes.act three behavior: He becomes a quiet anchor for the defense during deliberation.
